pain_point_label: Absence of Formal Internal Investigation Protocol

matches.match_rationale: The absence of a formal investigation protocol means no standardized templates, interview guides, evidence checklists, or reporting documentation exist. Text generation can produce a complete investigation protocol library — structured interview scripts for different parties (victim, witness, accused), step-by-step procedure documents, evidence preservation checklists, and formal reporting templates that align with mandatory reporting obligations. This directly addresses the mechanism: lack of codified process.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-drafted Investigation Protocol Pack: role-specific interview guides, evidence log templates, a chain-of-custody form, and a board-ready findings report template — all generated from safeguarding best practice inputs and localized to jurisdiction-specific mandatory reporting rules.

matches.match_rationale: When an abuse report is filed, there is no structured workflow — actions are ad hoc, unrecorded, and inconsistent. A task automation agent can enforce a mandatory multi-step investigation workflow: auto-log the report, trigger notifications to the correct oversight roles (not the accused's line manager), generate timestamped task assignments, enforce sequencing (no accused interview before victim statement is secured), and escalate to external authorities at defined checkpoints. This directly addresses the absence of impartial oversight and inconsistent authority handoffs.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A safeguarding case management agent that activates on report submission, sequences investigation steps, locks out conflicted parties, auto-generates documentation at each stage, and hard-triggers external reporting if internal resolution thresholds are not met within defined timeframes.

matches.match_rationale: Victims and witnesses are currently unsupported during investigations and may be interrogated chaotically by multiple staff. A trauma-informed conversational agent can provide a consistent, safe channel for victims and witnesses to give statements at their own pace, with standardized, non-leading questions — replacing ad hoc interrogation by untrained staff. The agent can document responses verbatim with timestamps, reducing re-traumatization and producing a defensible evidentiary record.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A trauma-informed disclosure agent that guides victims and witnesses through a structured, age-appropriate statement process via a secure interface, producing a timestamped verbatim record that feeds directly into the case file — removing untrained staff from the interview role.

matches.match_rationale: Evidence is not preserved and interviews are not documented. When any informal communications (emails, notes, messages) do exist, entity extraction can automatically identify and log key facts: dates, names, locations, incident descriptors, and involved parties — creating a structured evidentiary record from unstructured inputs. This addresses the evidence-not-preserved failure mode by capturing structured facts from whatever fragmented records do exist.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An evidence intake tool that ingests emails, written notes, or transcribed verbal reports and extracts structured case facts (parties, dates, locations, incident type) into a tamper-evident case log, ensuring no detail is lost to informal handling.

matches.match_rationale: Reports and communications related to safeguarding incidents vary in severity and urgency, and without a protocol, triage is absent. Text classification can automatically assess incoming reports for severity tier (immediate danger vs. historical disclosure vs. third-party concern), mandatory reporting trigger (yes/no), and case type — ensuring that critical cases are not buried or downgraded by uninformed staff decisions.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated report triage classifier that scores incoming safeguarding disclosures for severity, urgency, and mandatory-reporting obligation, routing them to the correct oversight level and preventing principals from self-managing cases that require external authority involvement.

matches.match_rationale: The current state leaves organizations blind to which cases represent ongoing risk to remaining students versus resolved historical disclosures. Risk scoring models can evaluate case characteristics (type of allegation, accused's access level, number of potential victims, prior reports) to produce a risk score that determines investigative priority and interim protective measures — directly addressing the 'failure to protect remaining students' causal factor.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A case risk stratifier that scores each reported incident on ongoing-danger probability, informing whether immediate suspension of accused, parent notification, or emergency authority referral is warranted — before the investigation formally begins.

matches.match_rationale: Investigators (or designated staff acting as investigators) have no reference to legal obligations, best-practice procedures, or prior case precedents. A RAG system grounded in jurisdiction-specific safeguarding legislation, mandatory reporting frameworks, and investigation best-practice guides can answer procedural questions in real time during a live investigation — preventing decisions made from ignorance of legal obligations.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A safeguarding compliance assistant that lets investigation leads query their jurisdiction's mandatory reporting rules, evidence handling requirements, and interview best practices in natural language, with cited source references, during an active case.
